changed: The packing checklist's checkboxes are now drawn by the same icon set as the toolbar's packing toggle — the same square-and-tick glyph, with the tick still popping in.
The big pack-weight total at the top of a list now reads larger.
fixed: On a shared list, an item's gear type now sits snug under its name, matching the editor.

added: Every item now carries a gear type like "tent" or "trail runners" — filled in automatically when you pick it, shown as a quiet line under the product, and renamable per list.
Start a new list straight from the editor's ⋯ menu.
changed: Typing a gear name now surfaces closer name matches first, above merely-popular ones.
Editing and packing modes now ease from one to the other when you switch, instead of snapping.
Empty folders no longer draw a faint line under their name — the divider only shows between rows once you've added items.
Editing an item's name now offers its gear type right below, so gear you type in yourself can carry one too.
CSV exports carry a "Gear Type" column; older files with a "Common Name" column still import.
A group row no longer offers a gear type of its own — the group's name already is one.
fixed: Switching to packing mode no longer makes the rows taller — editing and packing keep the same row height, so the list doesn't jump on toggle.
Item notes sit the same distance below their name in shared and public lists as they do in the editor.
The “Add an item” button now lines up with your list's row spacing on phone and desktop, instead of sitting a touch below it.
Nesting an item under a piece of gear that already has a weight now groups the two, so that gear's own weight stays on its own line — visible and editable — instead of vanishing into the group total.
Renaming a catalog item to your own name now clears the gear type that came with it, instead of leaving the old label stuck on the row for good.
Un-nesting an item out of a group the app created for you now hands its gear type back and removes the leftover group row.
Dragging one weighed item onto another no longer pops the keyboard open on a phone.
The gear type and note button now says what it will actually open, and opens to the field you asked for instead of the one already showing.
Screen readers and voice control can now reach an item's gear type and note button.
"Create a list" from the editor menu no longer drops the list you were on out of your browser history — Back returns to it.
Printed lists no longer show an "Add an item" line under each group.
A renamed catalog item now keeps tracking the catalog's gear type, and an item with your own gear type still tracks the catalog's product name.
A CSV column called "Type" is no longer imported as a gear type — only a "Gear Type" or "Common Name" column is.
An unnamed item no longer shows the placeholder label "Group" unless it really is a group.

added: A “What’s new” page — a running list of features and fixes as they ship.
Search the gear catalog by everyday name — type “tent” to find tents, or “rucksack” and “backpack” to reach the same packs.
Women’s versions across the gear catalog — about a hundred new entries covering footwear, apparel, packs, and sleep.
Nest items one level deep — split a tent into body, fly, poles, and stakes on their own lines, with the group total on the parent row.
Drag an item into or out of a nested group — drag it right to tuck it under the row above, left to pop it back out.
Collapse a group of nested items — a chevron by the group's name hides or shows its children (and remembers your choice), just like a folder.
changed: Combined the Privacy and Terms pages into a single Legal page.
On phones, an item's extra actions (add a note, nest, un-nest) tuck into a ⋯ menu, so each row stays uncluttered — delete and drag stay put.
fixed: Nested items now work in packing mode — you can check them off, and pack progress can reach 100%.
Dragging a grouped item into another folder now carries its nested items along, so weights stay correct.
Copying a list with nested items no longer leaves hidden phantom rows in the copy.
Shared and public lists now show folders in the same order you arranged them.
Reorder items and folders from the keyboard — focus the grip and press the up/down arrows.
Search an accented brand by its plain spelling — typing "Fjallraven" now finds Fjällräven (and Klattermusen finds Klättermusen).

added: Sort any folder by name, heaviest, or lightest — or keep your own manual order.
Import a JSON backup to restore a list. Exporting and re-importing now round-trips a list at full fidelity.
A quiet line in the editor tells you when your list last saved and synced.
fixed: The undo toast is easier to catch — it now stays for ten seconds, pauses when you hover or focus it, and no longer swallows a stray click as it fades.

added: “Your lists” remembers the lists you've opened on this device, and lets you delete one.
A “Copy this list” button on shared lists, so anyone can fork their own editable copy.
Import an existing list straight from a LighterPack link or a CSV file.
A print-friendly layout and a packing checklist with check-off progress, for using a list on the trail.
Add the same item a few times and it folds into one row with a count and a worn split, e.g. “Socks ×3 · 1 worn”.

title: Mahonia is live
added: Build a packing list and share it with a link — no account, no app, no ads.
Live pack-weight totals, with a base / worn / consumable breakdown and a category bar. Weights are optional; a list with none just stays quiet.
A curated, cited gear-weight database with autocomplete, so most items weigh themselves as you type.
Light and dark themes.
